python.name: WRDS Event Study (Polars + DuckDB)
python.summary: High-performance event study framework replicating the WRDS Python app, enhanced with Polars and DuckDB for ~240× acceleration.
features: Abnormal return models: market-adjusted, CAPM, FF3, Carhart4
Supports CAR, BHAR, abret with flexible event windows
Efficient processing of large-scale samples

python.name: GPT-Disclosure Analyzer
python.summary: Modular pipeline for analyzing SEC filings using OpenAI GPT models via keyword and item extraction.
features: Automatic extraction of selected SEC items (e.g., Item 1, 7 of 10-K)
Custom keyword filtering (unigrams and bigrams)
Batch annotation via OpenAI Chat API

python.name: Image-Text Inference with LLaVA
python.summary: Multimodal inference tool using LLaVA v1.5 for interpreting image + prompt input.
features: Supports remote and local image input
Runs efficiently on GPU with float16
Returns vision-language model responses

python.name: RavenPack News Processor
python.summary: Pipeline for processing RavenPack news event data with keyword filtering, accelerated by Polars and DuckDB.
features: Supports unigram and bigram keyword filtering
Polars + DuckDB for high performance
Outputs .parquet files with compression

python.name: FF Industry Parser
python.summary: Lightweight parser for mapping SIC codes to Fama-French industry categories.
features: Parses 49-industry (or 12) structure
No external dependencies (pure re module)

python.name: Replication of Donelson et al. (2011, TAR)
python.summary: Replicates and extends Donelson et al. (2011) using Compustat data, validated on 1967–2005 and extended to 2023.
features: Key financial ratios and rolling volatility metrics
Replicates Table 1 and Table 2
Extends analysis to 2006–2023

python.name: Replication of Daniel et al. (1997, JF)
python.summary: Constructs 125 characteristic-sorted portfolios (5×5×5) using size, book-to-market, and momentum quintiles.
features: NYSE breakpoints for size, BM, and momentum quintiles
12-2 month momentum window with minimum 8 valid observations
Multiple share class handling

python.name: Replication of Call et al. (2023, RAST)
python.summary: Replicates tone calculation from earnings call transcripts using the Loughran-McDonald sentiment dictionary.
features: Separates manager (type 4) and analyst (type 3) statements
Loughran-McDonald sentiment scoring
Outputs tone_manager and tone_analyst measures

r.name: Replication of García et al. (2023, JFE)
r.summary: Joint work with Miao Liu and Yang Cao at Boston College. Replicates and extends García et al. (2023) using updated conversation datasets and customized NLP models.
features: Full replication of original variable construction pipeline
FineTuneBert.py: fine-tunes Google BERT for earnings call prediction
BertShap.py: interprets BERT attention via Shapley values

sas.name: High-Frequency Event Study TAQ
sas.summary: Extracts short-window trading activity and quote information around event timestamps for market microstructure analysis.
features: Customizable event input with your own event dataset
Automatically detects and matches TAQ data files by date
